_doc: Retail-side field spec for the execution+dataflow trace (docs/plans/trace-tooling-phase-b.md, the B2 LOGIC drill-in). Maps each instrumented engine VA -> the declared payload the Frida agent reads at that call, joined to the port's CALL_TRACE_* fields by (va, field-name). Consumed by tools/flow_diff.py.

_schema.key: engine VA (0x-hex or decimal). Matches the port's CALL_TRACE_BEGIN(va).
_schema.name: short label for flow_diff output (wins over functions.csv).
_schema.chain_benign: true => exclude this VA's POSITION from chain alignment (a clock read / harness-substituted poll that legitimately floats). No field payload needed.
_schema.fields[].src: global (read DAT at `va`) | arg (Frida onEnter arg index) | argderef (deref arg[index] + `off`) | chain (global root + pointer hops) | rngcalls (cumulative LCG-draw count = the agent's g_rng_count_total; auto-installs the single FUN_005bf505 hook — the RNG-CONSUMPTION signal unified into the flow trace, no ad-hoc flag) | retval (onLeave).
_schema.fields[].va: for src=global/chain: the DAT_ VA to read (chain reads it as the root pointer).
_schema.fields[].index: for src=arg/argderef: Frida InvocationArguments index, 0-BASED (index 0 = first STACK param).
_schema.fields[].off: for src=argderef/chain: byte offset added before the final typed read.
_schema.fields[].hops: for src=chain: list of byte offsets, each a pointer hop after reading the global root pointer at `va`; the final `off` is the value read. chain = read *(va), then for each h: p = *(p + h), then read typed at p + off. Reaches a field behind a global root (e.g. the camera/view at *(*(DAT_008a9b50) + 0x104c) + off).
_schema.fields[].benign: true => compare for PRESENCE only (memory-layout pointer, phase-origin counter, RNG seed origin); pair with `reason`.
_schema.fields[].reason: human note surfaced by --field-timeline when a benign field diverges (accepted-with-cause, not silently dropped).

_caveat_arg_index: SotES is MSVC; many engine functions are __thiscall/__fastcall (this/first arg in ECX, not on the stack). For those, Frida's args[0] is the first STACK param, NOT `this` — validate any src=arg/argderef field against a live --textout-probe/mem_watch read before a port depends on it. src=global reads (a DAT_ VA) have no such ambiguity and are the safe default for state-machine stubs; prefer them. Validated convention will be pinned here per-VA as fields land.

0x5b8fc0.name: flip
0x5b8fc0._note: FUN_005b8fc0 — the DDraw Flip dispatcher, entered ONCE PER FRAME (it is the agent's flip-frame anchor). The right cross-side per-frame VA: retail's title runner FUN_0056aea0 keeps its do/while loop INTERNAL, so onEnter there fires once at scene entry, not per frame; the port externalized that loop, so the shared per-frame boundary both sides hit is the Flip. Port side: src/main.c drive_present (CALL_TRACE_BEGIN(0x5b8fc0)).

fields.name: rng
fields._note: DAT_008a4f94 — the engine LCG state word (srand FUN_005bf4fb seeds it; seeded to OSS_RNG_DEFAULT_SEED on both sides under --seed-pin). Read at the Flip = the frame-boundary RNG snapshot. A [data] divergence here the frame after a mismatched draw localizes a stray RNG consumption (parity pillar 3, docs/parity-model.md).

fields.name: rngcalls
fields.reason: cumulative LCG (FUN_005bf505) draw count at the Flip = per-frame RNG-consumption total. Benign for now (RNG order parity is deferred until every in-scene RNG consumer is RE'd, per the ckpt-73 decision); surfaced so flow_diff can localize WHERE consumption diverges once we match it 1:1.

fields.name: tint
fields.reason: DAT_008a93fc — the global palette-tint / time-of-day index FUN_00417c40 switches on (in_ECX+0x289c == &DAT_008a760c-0xaac+0x289c == 0x8a93fc). 0 = case-0 IDENTITY (no recolor); 1 = warm, 2/3 = darken, 4/5 = the FUN_004182d0 complex ramp. The port's tile/parallax resolve uses the plain getter 0x418470 (no tint); whether the in-game town needs the tint hinges on this value (PORT-DEBT render-palette-tint).

fields.name: lutgate1
fields.reason: in_ECX+0x29b0 — FUN_00417c40's secondary-LUT gate A. When (0x8a9510 != 0 || 0x8a9514 != 1000) the getter remaps every palette index through the 256-byte LUT at 0x8a9410 before returning the cel (the 'darker/more-saturated' color post the port's 0x418470 skips).

fields.name: lutgate2
fields.reason: in_ECX+0x29b4 — FUN_00417c40's secondary-LUT gate B (the 1000 = identity baseline; != 1000 arms the LUT).

fields.name: lut000
fields.reason: LUT bytes [0..3] (in_ECX+0x28b0). Identity LUT => 0x03020100. A non-identity value confirms an index remap is loaded.

fields.name: lut064
fields.reason: LUT bytes [0x40..0x43]; identity => 0x43424140.

fields.name: lut128
fields.reason: LUT bytes [0x80..0x83]; identity => 0x83828180.

fields.name: lut192
fields.reason: LUT bytes [0xc0..0xc3]; identity => 0xc3c2c1c0.

fields.name: cam_mapw
fields.reason: exploratory camera/view probe (in-game-intro.md): view[0x00] = map pixel width = dim0*0xc80, set at 586010:856. No port equivalent yet — retail ground-truth for the camera build.

fields.name: cam_maph
fields.reason: view[0x04] = map pixel height = dim1*0xc80, 586010:862.

fields.name: cam_x34
fields.reason: view+0x34 — scroll-x sub-pixel accumulator (projector sx term); zeroed at room init via 587d30, driven by the camera follow. The value at the first town frame tells us if the camera snapped to the spawn.

fields.name: cam_x4c
fields.reason: view+0x4c — scroll-y sub-pixel accumulator (projector sy term); zeroed at room init via 587d30, driven by the follow.

fields.name: cam_x5c
fields.reason: view+0x5c — y scroll origin (projector sy term); set 0 at 586010:858.

fields.name: cam_x60
fields.reason: view+0x60 — x scroll origin (projector sx + window col0); set 0 at 586010:857.

fields.name: cam_x64
fields.reason: view+0x64 — viewport width (window ncols, /0xc80); set 64000 (=640*100) at 586010:860.

fields.name: cam_x68
fields.reason: view+0x68 — viewport height (window nrows, /0xc80); set 48000 (=480*100) at 586010:861.

fields.name: cam_x74
fields.reason: view+0x74 — zoom/vertical-shear term (projector sy: +cam+0x74*100); set 0 at 586010:859, updated from a controller's +0x4c at 499ab0:96.

fields.name: cam_x1c
fields.reason: view+0x1c (view[7]) — camera pan state flag; 439690 zeroes it on a snap/pan command. Probing to model the pan.

fields.name: cam_x20
fields.reason: view+0x20 (view[8]) — the camera follow VELOCITY CAP (= pan speed); the easer FUN_0043d1d0 ramps vel +10/frame up to this. The town pan = 300 (set once with the target at hold-end). Ported: camera_view.cap.

fields.name: cam_x6c
fields.reason: view+0x6c (view[0x1b]) — TARGET x scroll origin (clamped to [0, mapw-vpw]); RESOLVED: a SCRIPTED constant (12800 for the town), set once at hold-end, NOT leader-tracking. The easer FUN_0043d1d0 moves +0x60 toward it. Ported: camera_view.tgt_x.

fields.name: cam_x70
fields.reason: view+0x70 (view[0x1c]) — TARGET y scroll origin (clamped to [0, maph-vph]); pairs with cam_x6c (12800, static through the town pan). Ported: camera_view.tgt_y.

0x43d1d0.name: camera_follow_step
0x43d1d0._note: FUN_0043d1d0 (366 B) — the once-per-frame camera EASE-TO-TARGET, called from 0x439690:1123 (then FUN_00499ab0 shake/HUD). Dispatched through a HEAP function pointer (invisible to static call-graph search); located ckpt 69 with a HW watchpoint on view+0x60 (writer insn 0x43d26d). Per axis: dist=|tgt-cur|; if vel<dist -> cur += sign*(vel + (flag&&dist>16000 ? (dist-16000)/10 : 0)), vel=min(vel+10,cap); else cur=tgt (snap), vel=max(vel-10,0). Then FUN_0043d340 writes the projector accumulators +0x34/+0x4c (shake; inert during the pan). PORT MIRROR: src/camera_follow.c camera_follow_step (host-tested bit-exact vs the captured retail trajectory); add CALL_TRACE_BEGIN(0x43d1d0) when it is wired into the live game_drive camera (PORT-DEBT ingame-camera-pan). Fields read at onEnter (the view = *(*(0x8a9b50)+0x104c)): the X-axis easer state.

fields.name: cur_x
fields.reason: view+0x60 — current x scroll (the easer's output; matches cam_x60 at the Flip). camera_view.cur_x.

fields.name: tgt_x
fields.reason: view+0x6c — target x scroll. camera_view.tgt_x.

fields.name: vel_x
fields.reason: view+0x08 — the X follow VELOCITY accumulator (+10/frame, cap=+0x20); previously unprobed, now the integrator. camera_view.vel_x.

fields.name: vel_y
fields.reason: view+0x0c — the Y follow velocity accumulator. camera_view.vel_y.

fields.name: cap
fields.reason: view+0x20 — velocity cap / pan speed (300 for the town). camera_view.cap (== cam_x20).

fields.name: flag
fields.reason: view+0x1c — far-boost enable (0 for the town pan -> no (dist-16000)/10 term). camera_view.flag (== cam_x1c).

0x46cd70.name: actor_update_all
0x46cd70._note: FUN_0046cd70 (1031 B) — the per-SIM-TICK actor-update master driver, called once per tick from the in-game loop 0x439690 as FUN_0046cd70(1) (when *(param+0x1c)==0). Walks every actor-pool band off DAT_008a9b50 (active = actor+0x1d0 != 0): 0x1160 (0x20), 0x1060 (0x40), 0x13e0 (0x400), 0x23e0 (0x60), and the MAIN band 0x11e0 (0x80=128 slots). For each main actor it ticks timers (+0x2c4, +0x2ec), then calls FUN_0054f980(actor+0x40, actor+0x40, 0, 0) for the primary render-state entry and FUN_0054f980(entry-0x294, entry, 1, idx) for each sub-entry (the kinematic body-part chain) — that is where the animation advances. This is the once-per-tick clock the actor anim rides; it is NOT the render/emit pass (FUN_0048c150). Determinism: the anim counter is a pure function of (sim-ticks since clip-set), so it pins on the SAME g_sim_tick anchor as the camera (game_enter scene-load reset) — engine-quirk #76. No port mirror yet (actors unported, PORT-DEBT present-actor-modes).

fields.name: frame_ctr
fields.reason: the god-object base pointer; presence-only. The per-tick frame counter +0x276c is bumped here (& 0x7fff) once per tick.

fields.name: rng
fields.reason: DAT_008a4f94 — the shared LCG state word, snapshotted at the per-SIM-TICK actor-update boundary (0x46cd70 onEnter). Determinism probe (ckpt 73): under --seed-pin the seed is identical both runs, so if the shared stream is consumed in the same ORDER per sim-tick, rng at tick N matches run-to-run. It does NOT (0/8643 ticks, quirk #77): a non-deterministic-rate consumer (a per-PRESENT draw x the non-deterministic presents-per-tick, quirk #75) perturbs the stream phase the per-tick actor draws then see — the ~6.7k-px actor-band residual (0x54f980 draws this LCG for idle waits + wander offsets). parity pillar 3, docs/parity-model.md.

fields.name: rngcalls
fields.reason: cumulative LCG draw count at the per-sim-tick actor-update boundary — the RNG-consumption signal unified into the flow trace (openrecet rngcalls pattern). Pairs with `rng` (state): once every in-scene RNG consumer is RE'd, matching rng+rngcalls per anchor on both sides is how the actor band reaches 1:1 (deferred per ckpt 73).

0x54f980.name: actor_update
0x54f980._note: FUN_0054f980 (11597 B) — the per-actor update / behaviour dispatch, called per render-state entry from FUN_0046cd70 (so once per SIM-TICK). It (1) shadow-copies the render-state param_1->param_2 (the kinematic chain: a sub-entry inherits its parent's transform), then (2) switches on the actor TYPE in_ECX[0x75] (= actor+0x1d4, the 0x1131e.. behaviour id). EVERY animating case runs the SAME inline frame-stepper on the render-state's anim fields (+0x6c clip / +0x70 timer / +0x72 frame / +0x74 done): timer++; if timer>=clip[+0x44](dur){ frame++; timer=0; if frame>=clip[+0x42](count){ clip[+0x48]==0 ? frame=clip[+0x152](loop_to) : (hold last: frame=count-1, done=1, timer=1) } }. The clip (+0x6c) is set on STATE CHANGE by FUN_0040afe0/FUN_0041e600 (reset timer/frame/done, but only when the clip actually changes). PORT MIRROR: src/anim_clip.c anim_clip_advance + anim_state_set (host-tested); the clip descriptor layout (count@0x42, dur@0x44, oneshot@0x48, loop_to@0x152, per-frame sprite delta + x/y offsets) is anim_clip. Fields below read actor SLOT 0's primary render-state anim block (representative). engine-quirk #76; findings/in-game-intro.md 'The actor animation cycle'.

fields.name: a0_clip
fields.reason: actor[0] render-state +0x6c — current animation clip ptr (anim_state.clip). 0 = inert.

fields.name: a0_timer
fields.reason: actor[0] render-state +0x70 — frame timer (low u16; sim-ticks in the current frame). anim_state.timer.

fields.name: a0_frame
fields.reason: actor[0] render-state +0x72 — current frame index (low u16). anim_state.frame; advances once per sim-tick, deterministic under the g_sim_tick anchor.
